MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jan-23-2026 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of August 20, 2025, Order Denying Motion To Compel
Off Calendar Oct-28-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows

PLAINTIFF JEFFREY SMITH , AN INDIVIDUAL, ONTHEGO, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, ONTHEGO WIRELESS, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LLC Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of August 20, 2025, Order Denying Motion To Compel

Plaintiffs Jeffrey Smith, et al. move for reconsideration of this court's order denying Smith's motion to compel defendant Hanson Bridgett LLP to produce documents and to compel Fred Weil to answer questions in response to Smith's deposition notice. Smith seeks reconsideration based on (1) the court's error in describing attorney Fred Weil's letter of August 24, 2012 in its order; (2) deposition testimony of Smith's counsel Mark James concerning his belief that Weil represented Smith; and (3) Smith's counsel's inability to respond fully to the questions the court provided to counsel in its tentative. The court grants reconsideration because it in fact misdescribed the letter in its prior order. However, nothing in the prior order turned on that error, and the court reaches the same conclusion upon further consideration. The court therefore grants reconsideration sua sponte, vacates the August 20, 2025 order, and replaces it with this order denying the motion to compel based on similar reasoning.

The motion to compel is denied.

(See word minutes for entire ruling)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-11-2024 Continued To Nov-13-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-24-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Garet D. O'Keefe, Esq., O'Keefe & O'Keefe LLP (510) 282-0298 garet@okeefelaw.com Attorney for Plaintiffs Jeffrey Smith, OnTheGo, Inc., and OnTheGo Wireless, LLC (via ZOOM)
Amy L. Bomse, Esq., ROGERS JOSEPH O'DONNELL 415.956.6457 abomse@rjo.com Attorney for Defendant HANSON BRIDGETT LLP

Hearing is held. Argued. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT HANSON BRIDGETT LLP DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT

Defendant Hanson Bridgett LLP's (HB) demurrer to the first amended complaint (FAC) in this legal malpractice case is overruled.

The demurrer has one ground: that the FAC's causes of action are all time-barred under CCP 340.6(a). (Dmr. 3:11-4:1.)

The FAC pleads that plaintiff Jeffrey Smith was a telecom consultant who discovered telecoms were overcharging government agencies and became a whistleblower, filing qui tam and other lawsuits. (FAC 2:26-3:24.) Seeking to fund his efforts, Smith was referred to Sameer Barakat, another whistleblower, who became Smith's consultant and later litigation foe. (FAC 3:18-4:12.) The FAC further pleads that HB, which had been Barakat's lawyers, also began representing Smith in June 2012, though HB was "secretly advancing Barakat's interests to Smith's detriment," as Smith later learned. (FAC 14:19-24 see also 4:23-14:18.)
HB's theory is that plaintiffs' claims are all barred under CCP 340.6(a)'s one-year limitation "after the plaintiff discovers, or through the use of reasonable diligence should have discovered, the facts constituting the wrongful act or omission." (Memo. 11:7-12:5; see also Opp. 10:19-26.) HB argues Smith should have discovered its wrongful acts because HB "ceased doing any work for him despite their promises of ongoing support." (Memo. 12:6-13:6.) But this could well have signaled inattentiveness insufficient to arouse suspicion of wrongdoing. The facts require further development; this is but the pleading stage.

Given the above, CCP 340.6 "actual injury" and continued-representation tolling need not be reached.

Proposed order submitted.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
